content.text:
<user_query>
Take a look at the way that the public pages app is doing styles with the way that it's set up with Tailwind, and try to apply that same sort of methodology to the org next app. We should look for making sure that all of the colors are the same in the CSS. Copy them over from the public pages app to the org next app. We should also do a quick audit through all of the styles in the org next app to make sure they're using as few custom styles (including spacing and colors) and as many of the CSS styles as possible, just for consistency.
</user_query>

Exploring Tailwind and CSS setup in the public-pages and org-next apps to align styling and audit custom styles.

[REDACTED]

Confirming `styles.css` matches public-pages (same imports and `@theme`). Aligning org-next components with semantic tokens from `@workspace/ui/theme.css` and replacing numbered `primary-*` scales and arbitrary values. Reading a few key files and applying systematic updates.

[REDACTED]

Applying semantic tokens from the shared theme (`foreground`, `muted-foreground`, `border`, `muted`, `accent`, `primary`, `destructive`) to match public-pages patterns. `styles.css` already matches public-pages. Updating components systematically.

[REDACTED]

content.text:
Here’s what we found and what we changed.

## Setup vs public-pages

`apps/org-next/src/styles.css` already matched `apps/public-pages/src/styles.css`: Tailwind v4 (`@import "tailwindcss"`), `tw-animate-css`, `@import "@workspace/ui/theme.css"`, the same `@source` globs, `@custom-variant dark`, the same `@theme` bridge (`--color-background`, `--color-foreground`, `brand-*`, etc.), and the same base layer (headings, links, `body`).

**Color source:** Both apps rely on **`packages/ui/src/styles/theme.css`** (exported as `@workspace/ui/theme.css`). There’s no separate palette to copy into org-next—the shared theme is the single source of truth.

**Build:** Both use `@tailwindcss/vite` in Vite (org-next uses port `3210`, public-pages `3207`; org-next also has TanStack devtools gated off—that’s unrelated to styling).

## Org-next UI audit (what we updated)

Across routes and components we moved from **numbered scales** (`primary-400`, `primary-800`, …) and **raw colors** (`red-*`, `emerald-*`, `amber-*`, …) to **semantic tokens** from the theme:

| Before (examples) | After (pattern) |
|-------------------|-----------------|
| `text-primary-800` / `text-primary-500` / `text-primary-400` | `text-foreground` / `text-muted-foreground` |
| `border-primary-100/200`, `divide-primary-*` | `border-border`, `divide-border` |
| `bg-primary-50`, `bg-white` (surfaces) | `bg-muted`, `bg-card`, `bg-background` |
| `bg-primary-100` (subtle fills) | `bg-accent` |
| Primary actions / spinners | `bg-primary`, `text-primary`, `text-primary-foreground` |
| Errors | `text-destructive`, `border-destructive/20`, `bg-destructive/10` |
| Success / positive / checks | `text-chart-2`, `bg-chart-2/10`, etc. |
| Warnings / draft / “amber” states | `text-chart-4`, `bg-chart-4/15` |
| Arbitrary font sizes (`text-[11px]`, `text-[13px]`, `text-[15px]`, `text-[10px]`) | `text-xs` / `text-sm` |
| `h-9.5` | `h-10` |

**Files touched:** `oauth/callback.tsx`, `oauth/start.tsx`, `logout.tsx`, `login.tsx`, `signup.tsx`, `app-header.tsx`, `components/ui/field.tsx`, `marketing-carousel.tsx`, `_authed/index.tsx`, `_authed/organizations.tsx`, `_authed/workspace/new.tsx`, `_authed/transactions.tsx`, `_authed/campaigns/$campaignId.tsx`, `_authed/campaigns/new.tsx`.

We **kept** explicit **`secondary-*`** and **`secondary-600`** where they’re clearly brand/accent (logo, toggles, org switcher chip, active onboarding step)—they still come from the same `@workspace/ui` theme.

`styles.css` was left as-is because it already matches public-pages and correctly wires the shared theme.

---

I couldn’t run `pnpm typecheck` here (environment Node is v16; pnpm expects ≥18). After upgrading Node locally, run `pnpm run typecheck` and `pnpm run check` under `apps/org-next` to confirm.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>
Try running type check again. I updated the version of node, but I'm not sure if your version is the same as the version in my terminal.
</user_query>

input.command: mise --version 2>/dev/null; mise ls 2>/dev/null | head -30; ls -la ~/.config/mise 2>/dev/null | head -5
input.description: Inspect mise installation and installed runtimes

input.command: cd /Users/duhl/git/ui && mise exec -- node -v && mise exec -- pnpm --filter org-next run typecheck
input.description: Run typecheck from repo root so mise.toml Node 25.3 applies

input.command: cd /Users/duhl/git/ui && mise x node@25.3.0 -- node -v && mise x node@25.3.0 pnpm@10.29.2 -- pnpm --filter org-next run typecheck
input.description: Pin Node 25.3 via mise x for typecheck

input.command: which mise; type mise; command -v node; ~/.local/share/mise/installs/node/25.3.0/bin/node -v 2>/dev/null || ls ~/.local/share/mise/installs/node/ 2>/dev/null | head
